name: Get-UpdateHealth
description: Checks for pending Windows updates using the Windows Update COM API. Flags mandatory updates as CRIT and optional updates as WARN.
symptoms: how do I check if Windows is up to date

name: Get-UpdateHistory
description: Reports Windows Update install history — last successful install date, recent failures with HResult codes, and a list of the 10 most recent entries.
symptoms: check if a specific patch was installed

name: Get-EndpointHealth
description: Comprehensive MSP diagnostic tool. Checks CPU, memory, disk, network, services, and uptime. Paste into a remote terminal session.
symptoms: first script to run on a new ticket

name: Get-EventLogExport
description: Exports System, Application, Security, and Setup event logs (last 48h) to a ZIP of rendered XML. Output goes to C:\Temp.
symptoms: need to send logs to a vendor
attach Windows logs to support case

name: Get-RecentChanges
description: Reports all notable changes on a Windows endpoint in the last 72 hours including updates, installs, driver changes, reboots, crashes, and logons.
symptoms: something changed and now it's broken
user says it stopped working recently
what happened on this PC yesterday

name: Get-SystemDetails
description: Reports OS edition, feature update version, build, architecture, hardware manufacturer and model, active user, install date, and uptime. Warns if uptime exceeds 14 days.
symptoms: what OS version is this PC running
need system info for a ticket
asset info for a support case

name: Get-GraphicsDriverHealth
description: Checks GPU driver version and date for all installed video controllers. Warns if any driver is older than 365 days. Works for NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, and any vendor.

name: Get-Win11Readiness
description: Checks Windows 11 upgrade readiness: TPM 2.0, Secure Boot, CPU generation, RAM (4 GB min), and disk space (64 GB min). Reports CRIT for hard blockers.
symptoms: can this PC upgrade to Windows 11
is this hardware compatible with Windows 11

name: Get-PrintHealth
description: Checks print spooler health, stuck jobs, and offline printer states. Run with -Fix to stop the spooler, clear job files, and restart.

flags.when: Jobs are stuck in the queue and not clearing automatically — stops the spooler, wipes job files, and restarts it.

name: Get-DiskHealth
description: Checks free space on all fixed drives (CRIT ≤5%, WARN ≤15%), temp folder bloat, and Recycle Bin size. Run with -Fix to clear temp files and empty the Recycle Bin.
symptoms: PC running slow due to disk
not enough space to install software

flags.when: Temp folder or Recycle Bin is bloated and you want to reclaim space immediately without manual cleanup.

name: Get-BrowserCacheHealth
description: Checks Chrome, Edge, and Firefox cache sizes across all user profiles. Warns if any browser cache exceeds 500 MB. Run with -Fix to clear all caches.
symptoms: Chrome using too much disk space

flags.when: Browser cache is bloated and causing slowness or storage pressure — clears all detected caches across every user profile on the device.

name: Get-NetworkDriveHealth
description: Reports mapped network drives and their connectivity state. Warns on disconnected drives. Run with -Fix to remove all mapped drives.

flags.when: Disconnected or stale drive mappings need to be wiped before remapping via login script or MDM policy.

name: Get-TimeSyncHealth
description: Checks Windows Time service health, NTP source, stratum, last sync time, and time offset. Run with -Fix to re-register w32time and sync to the fastest reachable AU/global NTP server.
symptoms: time out of sync on Windows
login failing due to time skew

flags.when: Time is drifting or the w32time service is broken — re-registers the service, sets a reliable NTP source, and forces an immediate sync.

name: Get-SecurityBaseline
description: Checks Windows security baseline: Secure Boot, TPM presence and readiness, Fast Startup state, and Windows Firewall profile status. Flags CRIT/WARN for any failures.

name: Get-DeviceJoinHealth
description: Checks device join health across Entra ID, domain, and hybrid environments. Reports join type, PRT status and age, device certificate expiry, MDM enrolment and last sync, domain secure channel, DC reachability, machine account password age, and Kerberos ticket availability.
symptoms: user getting access denied after domain join

name: Get-BitLockerHealth
description: Checks BitLocker encryption status and TPM health on the C: drive. Reports protection status, encryption method, disk mode, key protectors, PCR validation profile, TPM state, Secure Boot, and UEFI vs Legacy BIOS. Flags issues relevant to Intune compliance.
symptoms: N-central reports BitLocker off after ImmyBot

flags.when: N-central / N-able reports BitLocker as not enabled after ImmyBot maintenance

name: Get-LocalUserHealth
description: Reports all local user accounts, enabled state, admin membership, password expiry, and last logon. Run with -Fix -Username <name> to create/enable a local admin account with non-expiring password.
symptoms: user locked out of local account
who has local admin on this device

flags.when: You need to create or re-enable a local admin account — typically for emergency access or a break-glass account when normal login paths are unavailable.

name: Get-OutlookHealth
description: Checks Outlook process state, install path, DNS resolution for outlook.office365.com, and internet connectivity. Flags missing install and DNS/connectivity failures.

name: Get-NetskopeHealth
description: Checks Netskope client installation state, version, agent service status, and certificate presence. Run with -Remove to fully uninstall Netskope including service, MSI, folders, certs, and roaming data.

name: Get-BatteryHealth
description: Checks battery health, capacity %, cycle count, charge state, and adapter info. Parses powercfg /batteryreport for capacity trend and flat-discharge history. Handles multi-battery devices and desktop/VM gracefully.
symptoms: how many charge cycles on this battery

name: get-printer-health
description: Checks CUPS daemon state, printer states (idle/stopped/error), stuck print jobs, default printer, and print sharing. Run with --fix to cancel all queued jobs, re-enable stopped printers, and restart the CUPS daemon.

flags.when: Jobs are stuck in the queue and not clearing — cancels all queued jobs, re-enables stopped printers, and restarts the CUPS daemon.

name: get-security-baseline
description: Checks the macOS security baseline: SIP, Gatekeeper, Firewall, FileVault, Secure Boot (T2 and Apple Silicon), XProtect and MRT versions, and remote access state (SSH, Screen Sharing). Flags anything that deviates from a hardened managed endpoint.

name: get-update-health
description: Checks pending macOS updates by name, whether a restart is required, last successful install date, and recent update history. Flags the restart-required + long-uptime combination as CRIT. softwareupdate runs in the background to keep total time under 30s.
symptoms: Mac needs a restart for updates
how long since last update on Mac

name: get-battery-health
description: Checks battery health, condition label (Normal / Replace Soon / Replace Now / Service Battery), capacity %, cycle count, temperature, charge state, adapter info, and recent low-battery events from the power log. Handles desktop Macs gracefully.
symptoms: how many cycles on MacBook battery

name: get-disk-health
description: Checks APFS container free space, swap usage, SMART health and sector counts, Time Machine backup age, and live I/O load. Flags the high-swap + low-disk combination that causes most macOS performance complaints. Run with --deep to also verify the APFS container for filesystem corruption (adds 20-60s).
symptoms: Mac performance degraded due to disk

flags.when: User reports data corruption, unexpected crashes, or disk errors in Console — runs diskutil verifyContainer to check for APFS filesystem corruption (adds 20–60s).

name: get-filevault-health
description: Checks FileVault encryption status, recovery key type (personal or institutional), MDM escrow profile presence, and Secure Token state for the logged-in user. Flags missing keys, deferred encryption, and escrow not configured.

name: get-mdm-health
description: Checks MDM enrolment health across Intune, Kandji, and Jamf. Reports enrolment status, provider detection, supervision state, bootstrap token escrow (Apple Silicon), and provider-specific agent health. Flags Jamf presence as a phase-out advisory.

name: get-EndpointHealth
description: Broad endpoint health check for macOS. Runs all checks in parallel — disk space, CPU load, RAM availability, pending updates, SMART disk health, AV/EDR presence, critical system services, and battery capacity. Outputs findings and detail blocks sized for ticket screenshots.
symptoms: first script to run on a Mac ticket
user says Mac is slow or broken

name: get-recent-changes
description: Reports all notable changes on a macOS endpoint in the last 72 hours — software installs, reboots, kernel panics, application crashes, new LaunchDaemons/Agents, driver changes, and user logons. Outputs a FINDINGS block, a categorised change timeline, and a plain-text ticket note.
symptoms: something changed on Mac and now it's broken
software installed on Mac without permission
what happened on this Mac yesterday

name: get-hostname-health
description: Checks macOS hostname consistency across ComputerName, HostName, and LocalHostName. Flags .local suffix drift caused by Bluetooth or AirDrop activity, unset HostName, and mismatches between values. Run with --fix to strip .local suffixes automatically.
symptoms: Mac showing wrong name on network
Mac name changed after AirDrop use

flags.when: HostName or LocalHostName has drifted with a .local suffix — strips the suffix so all three hostname values are consistent.

name: getCalPerms
description: Retrieves calendar permissions for a mailbox via the Microsoft Graph API using client credentials.
symptoms: who has access to this calendar
check who can see a user's calendar
user can't see another user's calendar

name: getUserGUID
description: Looks up a user's Object ID / GUID from Microsoft Graph by UPN.
symptoms: need the object ID for a user
find user GUID in Entra ID
what is the GUID for this user
user object ID for Graph API call

name: listUsers
description: Lists all users in a tenant via the Microsoft Graph API using OAuth client credentials flow.
symptoms: get a list of all users in the tenant
export user list from Entra ID
audit all accounts in a tenant

name: TenantDataExport
description: Template script to export M365 tenant data (users, Exchange, OneDrive, SharePoint, licenses) to an Excel workbook.
symptoms: need a spreadsheet of all M365 data
config_note: Template script — review and configure before running. Requires ImportExcel module.

name: ConfigAPI_Test
description: Tests connectivity to the 3CX Configuration API using the /xapi/v1/Defs endpoint with Bearer token auth.
config_note: Set $BaseUrl, $ClientId, and $ApiKey at the top of the script before running.

name: get3CXVersion
description: Authenticates to a 3CX PBX via OAuth client credentials and retrieves the current PBX version.
